mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals accelerating consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive in the majority of months with positive RSAFS momentum over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days when the threshold is active.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) confirm a tightening labor market that fuels wage-driven consumption growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days only when both series move in concert.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to outperform the individual PAYEMS signal alone in predicting positive 20-day returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating inflation, increases in the federal funds rate reduce real equity returns by tightening financial conditions; this negative effect on S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days is absent outside of high inflation regimes.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL 3-month change exceeds 0.5%, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns following FEDFUNDS increases are not significantly negative compared to stable inflation periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in fewer than 50% of months when INDPRO contracts over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strength in residential investment and construction employment, which amplifies broad economic growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are not positive during periods of rising HOUST over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.2 percentage points over two months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp in 2 months over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive surprises in real GDP growth (GDPC1) relative to the prior quarter drive upward revisions to earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly releases of GDPC1 growth fail to exhibit positive rank correlation with subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.03).
